title: Co-Labs
text:
See ML Systems in Action
  
    33 interactive labs powered by MLSys·IM. Each lab runs in your browser
    with live instruments, prediction locks, and reactive simulations.
    No installation required.
  
  
    
      33
      Interactive Labs
    
    
      2
      Volumes
    
    
      22
      Systems Walls
    
    
      0
      Labs Completed
    
  
  Browse Labs ↓








  
    1
    Click a Lab
    Choose from 33 labs across two volumes
  
  
    2
    It Runs in Your Browser
    Powered by Marimo + WebAssembly — zero setup
  
  
    3
    Your Progress Saves
    The Design Ledger tracks decisions across all labs
  






  🎯
  
    Start Here · Orientation
    The Architect's Portal
    
      UI orientation, Design Ledger setup, and deployment context selection.
      Complete this before any other lab.
    
  
  ~15 min





Volume I: Foundations 16 labs · Single-machine ML systems



  
    I
    Foundations
    The D·A·M Triad, Iron Law, and deployment spectrum
  
  

    
      
        Lab 01
        Memory Wall
      
      The AI Triad
      The D·A·M Triad and the 9-order-of-magnitude gap between H100 and Cortex-M7.
      ~45 minLab 00
      
        Core Question
        If the same model deployed to an H100, a Jetson, and an ESP32 fails for three different physical reasons — how do you diagnose which axis to fix before spending the budget?
      
    

    
      
        Lab 02
        Memory Wall
      
      The Iron Law
      Physics of performance: T = D/BW + O/R + L applied to real hardware.
      ~55 minLab 01
      
        Core Question
        When the Iron Law says your H100 is memory-bound at batch=1, what happens when you double compute throughput — and why doesn't it help?
      
    

    
      
        Lab 03
        Ops Wall
      
      The Silent Degradation Loop
      MLOps feedback loops and iteration velocity tradeoffs.
      ~45 minLab 02
      
        Core Question
        If no code has changed but accuracy dropped 13% in production, where in the ML workflow did the system silently fail?
      
    

    
      
        Lab 04
        Data Wall
      
      The Data Gravity Trap
      Pipeline bottlenecks where data volume dominates compute cost.
      ~50 minLab 03
      
        Core Question
        When moving 10 TB of training data costs more than the GPU hours to process it, how do you restructure the pipeline?
      
    

  




  
    II
    Build
    Neural compute, architectures, frameworks, and training
  
  

    
      
        Lab 05
        Memory Wall
      
      The Activation Tax
      Activation memory cost and the memory hierarchy's impact on neural compute.
      ~50 minLab 02
    

    
      
        Lab 06
        Compute Wall
      
      The Quadratic Wall
      Transformer attention's O(n²) cost and architectural bottlenecks.
      ~55 minLab 05
    

    
      
        Lab 07
        Compute Wall
      
      The Kernel Fusion Dividend
      Kernel fusion, dispatch overhead, and why frameworks matter.
      ~50 minLab 06
    

    
      
        Lab 08
        Memory Wall
      
      The Training Memory Budget
      Memory = weights + gradients + optimizer + activations. Where does it all go?
      ~55 minLab 05
    

  




  
    III
    Optimize
    Compression, hardware utilization, and the efficiency frontier
  
  

    
      
        Lab 09
        Data Wall
      
      The Data Selection Tradeoff
      Diminishing returns in curriculum learning and selection cost.
      ~45 minLab 04
    

    
      
        Lab 10
        Memory Wall
      
      The Compression Frontier
      Quantization and pruning Pareto frontier — accuracy vs. efficiency.
      ~55 minLab 08
    

    
      
        Lab 11
        Memory Wall
      
      The Roofline
      Roofline model, ridge point, and MFU — the memory wall in action.
      ~60 minLab 02, Lab 05
    

  




  
    IV
    Deploy
    Benchmarking, serving, and production operations
  
  

    
      
        Lab 12
        Compute Wall
      
      The Speedup Ceiling
      Amdahl's Law, benchmark validity, and the limits of parallelism.
      ~50 minLab 11
    

    
      
        Lab 13
        Latency Wall
      
      The Tail Latency Trap
      Little's Law, P99 vs average latency, and the batching paradox.
      ~55 minLab 11, Lab 12
    

    
      
        Lab 14
        Ops Wall
      
      The Silent Degradation Problem
      Drift detection, retraining cost, and when models silently fail.
      ~50 minLab 03, Lab 13
    

  




  
    ★
    Capstone
    Synthesis and responsible engineering
  
  

    
      
        Lab 15
        Fairness Wall
      
      There Is No Free Fairness
      The fairness-accuracy tradeoff — quantifying the cost of responsible design.
      ~50 minLab 10
    

    
      
        Lab 16
        Synthesis
      
      The Architect's Audit
      Full Design Ledger review — synthesize decisions across all Volume I labs.
      ~70 minCapstone
    

  





Volume II: At Scale 16 labs · Distributed ML systems



  
    I
    Foundations
    Scale illusion, compute infrastructure, communication, and data pipelines
  
  

    
      
        Lab 01
        Fleet Wall
      
      The Scale Illusion
      1000x hardware ≠ 1000x speedup. Fleet reliability, coordination tax, and the C-Cubed diagnostic.
      ~50 min
    

    
      
        Lab 02
        Memory Wall
      
      The Compute Infrastructure Wall
      The Memory Wall, Roofline at fleet scale, and the bandwidth staircase from NVLink to Ethernet.
      ~55 minVol II Lab 01
    

    
      
        Lab 03
        Latency Wall
      
      Communication at Scale
      The alpha-beta model, collective operations, and network topology tradeoffs.
      ~55 minVol II Lab 02
    

    
      
        Lab 04
        Data Wall
      
      The Data Pipeline Wall
      The storage-compute chasm, pipeline equation, and the birthday problem at datacenter scale.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 01
    

  




  
    II
    Build
    Distributed training, fault tolerance, and fleet orchestration
  
  

    
      
        Lab 05
        Compute Wall
      
      The Parallelism Puzzle
      Data parallel, ZeRO, pipeline parallel — each solves one constraint while creating another.
      ~55 minVol II Lab 03
    

    
      
        Lab 06
        Fleet Wall
      
      When Failure Is Routine
      Young-Daly optimal checkpointing, the checkpoint storm, and serving fault tolerance.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 05
    

    
      
        Lab 07
        Fleet Wall
      
      The Scheduling Trap
      Pollaczek-Khinchine queuing, gang scheduling fragmentation, and the utilization paradox.
      ~55 minVol II Lab 06
    

    
      
        Lab 08
        Memory Wall
      
      The Inference Economy
      Serving cost eclipses training within weeks. The KV cache wall and continuous batching.
      ~55 minVol II Lab 05
    

  




  
    III
    Optimize
    Performance engineering, edge deployment, and fleet operations
  
  

    
      
        Lab 09
        Compute Wall
      
      The Optimization Trap
      Roofline diagnostic, kernel fusion dividend, FlashAttention, and the optimization playbook.
      ~55 minVol II Lab 08
    

    
      
        Lab 10
        Thermal Wall
      
      The Edge Thermodynamics Lab
      Memory amplification tax, adaptation strategies, battery drain, and the federation paradox.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 02
    

    
      
        Lab 11
        Ops Wall
      
      The Silent Fleet
      O(N²) complexity explosion, silent failure tax, platform ROI, and alert fatigue.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 07
    

  




  
    IV
    Deploy
    Security, robustness, sustainability, and responsible AI
  
  

    
      
        Lab 12
        Privacy Wall
      
      The Price of Privacy
      Privacy scaling wall, privacy-accuracy frontier, defense overhead, and budget depletion.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 08
    

    
      
        Lab 13
        Robustness Wall
      
      The Robustness Budget
      Robustness tax, distribution drift, defense stack, and the compression-robustness collision.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 09
    

    
      
        Lab 14
        Carbon Wall
      
      The Carbon Budget
      The energy wall, geography of carbon, lifecycle shift, and the Jevons trap.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 11
    

  




  
    ★
    Capstone
    Fairness, fleet synthesis, and the final audit
  
  

    
      
        Lab 15
        Fairness Wall
      
      The Fairness Budget
      The Impossibility Theorem — DP, Equalized Odds, and Calibration cannot all hold.
      ~50 minVol II Lab 12
    

    
      
        Lab 16
        Synthesis
      
      The Fleet Synthesis
      Full Vol II synthesis — sensitivity wall, failure budget, and the fleet architecture blueprint.
      ~70 minCapstone
    

  






  The Design Ledger
  
    Every lab asks you to make predictions and design decisions. The Design Ledger
    saves your choices in your browser and carries them across all 33 labs — from Lab 00
    through the final capstone. Lab 08's training memory budget builds on Lab 05's activation
    analysis, which builds on Lab 01's magnitude calibration.
  
  
    
      --
      Your Track
    
    
      0
      Labs Done
    
    
      33
      Remaining
    
  
  
    Your ledger is personal. It lives in your browser's local storage.
    If you switch devices, you can export and import it from within any lab.
  






  Advanced: Run Locally
  For the full experience with MLSys·IM's physics engine, run labs locally:
  pip install mlsysim[labs]
git clone https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book.git
cd cs249r_book/labs
marimo run vol1/lab_01_ml_intro.py



  Part of the Machine Learning Systems textbook.
  
  Share feedback · View source











 Back to top
